title: 401 unauthorized on every API call after IdP cert rotation
description: Customer 'Helio Inc' reports all API calls returning 401 since 09:00. They rotated their Okta SAML cert at 08:55. Internal SDK is still validating against old JWKS cache.
gold_root_cause: Customer rotated their IdP SAML certificate but their internal SDK is caching JWKS for 24h. Stale cache rejects new token signatures.
gold_remediation: Customer admin must clear SDK JWKS cache or wait TTL. Educate on shorter cache TTL configuration.
resolution: Walked customer through cache flush. APIs accepting tokens within 10 minutes. SDK upgrade recommended to v3.4 which respects JWKS cache headers.

title: 429 rate-limit surge starting 17:48, ~120 in 10 minutes
description: VC-Observability alert TENANT-429-RATE fired at 17:51 with 429 count of 64 in last 10 minutes, climbing. Customer 'NorthRange Logistics' (TEN-NORTHRANGE-2023). Spread across multiple endpoints.
gold_root_cause: Customer-side retry storm. Their nightly batch job hit a transient 502 at 17:30, retry-without-backoff loop kicked in and saturated their per-tenant limit.
gold_remediation: Recommend exponential backoff and SDK upgrade. Confirm batch job pattern. No platform action.
resolution: Engaged customer engineering. Retry storm confirmed. Customer rolled out backoff fix. 429 rate normalised by 18:14. SDK upgrade ticket logged with customer.

title: Webhook delivery degradation, NACK rate 1.8% on primary pool
description: WHK-WORKER-LATENCY-HIGH fired at 14:08 on primary delivery pool. NACK rate climbed past 1% within 90 seconds. Standby pool healthy per linkhealth output. Status page shows no public incident yet.
gold_root_cause: Primary delivery pool degraded due to upstream worker capacity issue. Failover to standby authorised but blocked by expired TLS certificate on standby pool.
gold_remediation: Authorise failover to standby. If certificate fails, emergency rotation per RB-SEC-12.
resolution: Failover initiated 14:14. Cert expiry on standby blocked clean failover until 14:31. Backlog drained 14:38-15:00. PIR scheduled, see INC001.

title: WHK-RETRY-EXHAUSTED on single endpoint, customer Helio Inc
description: VC-Webhooks alert RETRY-EXHAUSTED fired on customer 'Helio Inc' (TEN-HELIO-2024). Single endpoint /webhooks/orders has been retrying for 24h on all 8 attempts. Endpoint returns 502.
gold_root_cause: Customer-side endpoint outage. Their staging endpoint was registered in production webhook config by mistake. Staging is down for the weekend.
gold_remediation: Customer admin updates endpoint URL to production. Replay-by-time once endpoint healthy. No platform action.
resolution: Customer engineering confirmed staging endpoint mistake. URL updated. Replay-by-time of last 24h queued. 142 events redelivered successfully.

title: User reports access to records they should not see, possible cross-tenant
description: Customer admin at 'Cordwell Pharma' (TEN-CORDWELL-2022) reports an internal user can see records labelled with another tenant's ID. Customer is concerned about cross-tenant exposure.
gold_root_cause: Service account configured legitimately across two tenants by the customer's parent company group structure. Records were correctly scoped, but UI displayed parent-group label which the user mistook for cross-tenant.
gold_remediation: Pull VC-Identity audit log. Confirm denial-or-grant pattern. Engage Privacy if any genuine cross-tenant access. Customer-facing language drafted by Privacy.
resolution: Audit log confirmed all access was within service-account-permitted scope. No cross-tenant exposure. UI label clarified with customer admin. Customer satisfied. Privacy notified for record-keeping but no action.

title: Cross-product sync lag climbing, real-time dashboards stale
description: VC-DATAPLATFORM-LATENCY-HIGH and SYNC-LAG-HIGH alerts active since 17:30. Sync lag CRM-to-Analytics climbing from 35s baseline towards 28 minutes peak. 47 enterprise tenants affected.
gold_root_cause: Sync queue back-pressure caused by VC-DataPlatform latency. Q1 reporting batch was running on live production cluster contrary to ringfencing policy.
gold_remediation: Scale sync worker pool per RB011. Investigate VC-DataPlatform batch contention. Notify VP Customer Support and DPO if SLA breach trajectory crosses threshold.
resolution: Worker pool scaled at 18:03. Reporting batch killed at 18:48. Lag back to baseline by 19:15. Near-miss on 24h-average SLA. PIR scheduled, see INC002.

title: Spike in WHK-SIG-MISMATCH errors, ~80 in 30 minutes, customer Northwave
description: VC-Webhooks alert WHK-SIG-MISMATCH-RATE fired at 08:55. Customer 'Northwave Tech' (TEN-NORTHWAVE-2025) seeing 80+ signature failures in 30 minutes. All other customers fine.
gold_root_cause: Customer rotated webhook signing secret in their VertexCloud admin console at 08:50 but did not push the new secret to their receiver application until 10:00. Mismatch window of 70 minutes.
gold_remediation: Confirm with customer admin. Recommend coordinated rotation procedure (overlap window, dual-secret support). No platform action.
resolution: Customer engineering confirmed rotation timing issue. New secret deployed at 09:38. Signature errors stopped at 09:42. Goodwill replay of failed events queued. Coordinated-rotation guide shared with customer.

title: Both API gateway regions elevated latency, ~280ms p95, no errors yet
description: VC-API-GATEWAY-LATENCY-HIGH fired on EU region at 13:44, then US region 90 seconds later. p95 on both ~280ms vs 80ms baseline. Error rate normal. No public incident on status page.
gold_root_cause: Cloud provider maintenance event affecting both regions of VertexCloud's underlying CDN. Provider confirmed 12 minutes after symptom start. Resolved by them.
gold_remediation: Hold; communicate via incident bridge. VertexCloud cannot remediate provider directly. Customer Success prep proactive comms language for affected customers.
resolution: Cloud provider confirmed maintenance event at 13:56, completed at 14:08. Latency normalised by 14:18. No customer-impacting errors. Proactive comms not sent.

title: Multiple customer reports of duplicate records in Analytics Cloud after SDK upgrade
description: Contact Centre escalated 6 customer reports in 90 minutes, all from customers running SDK v4.18, all reporting duplicate rows in Analytics Cloud target. VC-Sync dedup engine did not catch any.
gold_root_cause: SDK v4.18 introduced bug where dedup-key was regenerated on retry rather than reused. Different keys, identical content. VC-Sync dedup engine could not detect.
gold_remediation: Page Data Platform Engineering. Hotfix or roll back SDK release. Reconciliation team de-duplicates affected records. Authorisation: Reconciliation Manager.
resolution: Engineering rolled back SDK v4.18 to v4.17.3 at 16:02. Reconciliation processed for 6 reported customers plus 9 more identified post-rollback. Total 15 customers affected, all de-duplicated within 24 hours.

title: Customer integration broken, 403 PERMISSION_DENIED on /v2/cases endpoint
description: Customer 'Stagecoach Retail' (TEN-STAGECOACH-2021) reports their case-creation integration started returning 403 today at 09:00. Single endpoint, single integration.
gold_root_cause: Customer admin removed 'cases:write' scope from the OAuth client used by the integration during a routine permissions audit on 03-12. Did not inform integration owner.
gold_remediation: Standard customer comms. Customer admin re-grants scope. No retry needed once scope restored.
resolution: Customer admin re-granted scope at 11:30. Integration recovered. Communicated permission-audit-coordination guidance to customer.
